stimulus: Marble Garden is a 1995 sculpture by the Italian-born artist Gabriella Russo. Like many of Russo’s sculptures, Marble Garden, which arranges thirty hand-carved flowers in a single bed, attempts to evoke every season simultaneously in a single ______ early-spring crocuses bloom beside late-summer sunflowers, and frost-tinged autumn leaves drift against the stems of winter hellebores.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice C is correct. The colon correctly introduces the following description elaborating how the sculpture evokes every season simultaneously — namely by combining crocuses, sunflowers, autumn leaves, and hellebores.

Choice A is incorrect because the result is a run-on; the two main clauses ("Like…composition" and "while…hellebores") are fused without proper punctuation, and "while" misrepresents the relationship. Choice B is incorrect because it produces a run-on, fusing the two clauses without any punctuation. Choice D is incorrect because the conjunction "while" suggests simultaneity or contrast and fails to signal that what follows is an explanation of the prior clause.
